name: Orbit Notes
oneLineDescription: Lightweight team workspace for capturing meeting notes, decisions, and action items
whatItIs: Orbit Notes is a lightweight team workspace for capturing meeting notes, decisions, and action items. It helps small teams turn messy conversations into clear next steps. The primary use case is a team lead creates a note during or after a meeting, captures key decisions, assigns action items to teammates, and everyone can later search, filter, and track progress.
coreUserFlows: Team lead creates a note during or after a meeting and captures key decisions
Team lead assigns action items to teammates from within notes
Team members search, filter, and track progress on action items
Users search across all notes using keywords to find relevant information

dataModel.purpose: Stores user accounts with team membership and roles
Containers for organizing notes within teams
Core content units capturing meeting discussions and decisions
Tasks extracted from notes that can be assigned and tracked

crossCuttingPrinciples: Three-tier authorization model with Viewer, Contributor, and Team Lead roles with increasing permissions
Single-team workspaces where users belong to exactly one team and all data access is team-scoped
A team must always have at least one Team Lead
All notes must belong to a project and all action items must belong to a note
Rich text support limited to headings, bold, italic, strikethrough, bullet lists, and numbered lists
specSummary: Orbit Notes is a team workspace designed to transform messy meeting conversations into organized, actionable outcomes. Built on Next.js 14 with Supabase backend, it serves small teams who need to capture meeting notes, track decisions, and manage action items without complexity. The application centers around a hierarchical content model where teams contain projects, projects contain notes, and notes contain action items. Users operate under a three-tier permission system (Viewer, Contributor, Team Lead) that governs access to creation, editing, and management functions. Core functionality includes rich text note creation with basic formatting, project-based organization, task assignment and tracking, keyword search across content, and activity timeline visualization. The system enforces strict data isolation per team, ensuring users only access their team's content. Technical constraints include responsive web-only delivery, basic rich text formatting without advanced features like file attachments or real-time collaboration, and performance targets like sub-second search results for teams with up to 1,000 notes. The build follows a dependency chain starting with authentication and team foundation, then project management, notes, action items, search, activity timeline, and team settings, ensuring each feature builds properly on its prerequisites.
